rulings.Case Title: JAMES SCHELSKE VS. BORIS WAYNE ABRAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Pltf To Attend Deposition And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT BORIS ABRAM, TRANS METRO EXPRESS Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Attend Deposition And For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant moves to compel the deposition of the plaintiff after two attempts to schedule same by notice. On both occasions plaintiff¿s attorney advised that the plaintiff could not be located. The motion is unopposed. Trial is scheduled for July 7, 2014. Plaintiff is ordered to appear for deposition within 30 days from the date of this order. The request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. DEMAS YAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Why Receiver Should Not Be Appointed
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, Line 2, Order To Show Cause Why Receiver Should Not Be Appointed. The Court finds that a receiver shall not be appointed at this time. On January 27, 2014, the Court granted Defendant's motion for reconsideration of the Court's December 18, 2013 order, in which Defendant's transfer of his interest in 547 23rd Avenue LLC ("the LLC") was set aside. The January 27, 2014 Order charges Defendant's interest in the LLC, if he has any. However, Defendant's opposition to the motion to charge his interest in the LLC and Defendant's opposition to the order to show cause why receiver should not be appointed state that Defendant no longer has any interest in the LLC because he transferred all of his shares to his relatives. Plaintiff has not offered evidence demonstrating that Defendant currently has an interest in the LLC. Therefore, a receiver cannot be appointed to manage an LLC that Defendant currently has no interest in. rulings.Case Title: MARLON DULAY VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hrg On Petition For Alt Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, Line 3, PETITIONER MARLON DULAY'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. The petition for writ of mandate is denied. Respondent maintained its initial burden of showing that Petitioner's BAC exceeded .08% under Petricka v. DMV (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 1341 and Baker v. Gourley (2000) 81 Cal.App.4th 1167, 1172-1173. The presumption of Evidence Code section 664 applies and satisfied the DMV's initial burden of proof in this case. The officer in compliance with an official duty provided his sworn report on form DS 367 with the results of the test as required by Vehicle Code sections 13380 and 23612. The burden then shifted to Petitioner to show that the officer did not carry out his official duties. Petitioner showed that Officer Werle reported the tests to the third decimal place in violation of 17 CCR 1220.4 and that he signed the wrong box on the form. Those errors were de minimus and are not the type of affirmative evidence necessary to show that Officer Werele failed to observe the official standards. The hearing officer did not err in finding Mr. Zehnder's testimony speculative and Petitioner failed to show that the administrative findings are contrary to the weight of the evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROGER S. CHAN MD VS. MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF CONSUMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Of Hearing On Writ Of Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, Line 4, PETITIONER ROGER CHAN'S Hearing On Writ Of Mandamus. Denied. The Court denies Petitioner's request to include the July 26, 2013, Clarification of PACE Physician Assessment of Roger Chan, M.D ("Clarification") document to the Administrative Record. (Petitioner's Exhibits to Petition for Alternative Writ of Mandate, Exh. 11-C.) Petitioner has not shown that he exercised reasonable diligence in attempting to produce the Clarification document at the administrative hearing. (CCP § 1094.5(e).) Even if the Court considered the Clarification document, upon independent review, the weight of the evidence supports the Administrative Law Judge's findings. A strong presumption of correctness is afforded to the Administrative Law Judge's findings. (Fukuda v. City of Angels (1999) 20 Cal.4th 805, 817.) The evidence shows that Respondent had legal grounds to discipline Petitioner under Business & Professions Code § 2305. (Administrative Record, Exh. 1.) Further, the weight of the evidence supports the finding that Respondent's disciplinary requirements are consistent with its goals to "aid in the rehabilitation of the licensee." (Business & Professions Code § 2229.) rulings.Case Title: GARY STRENG VS. KEYNOTE PROPERTIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Order Compelling Answers To Deposition Questions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, line 3, Plaintiff's Motion For Order Compelling Answers To Deposition Questions Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff¿s motion to compel answers to certain oral deposition questions is granted to the extent they should be responded to, in writing, in a verified pleading within twenty days. The questions posed were certainly proper if they had been asked with specificity to the time-frame of the events giving rise to the lawsuit. In the form asked they sought a present statement of the witnesses¿ understanding of terms. However, in their overall context they referenced the time-frame of the allegedly actionable events. Counsel for the Defendant should not have instructed the witness to not respond to the inquiry. Instructing a witness not to answer a deposition question on any ground other than privilege is improper. See: Stewart v. Colonial Western Agency (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 1006. Sanctions in the sum of $750.00 are awarded to plaintiff as against the defendant¿s counsel to be paid within 30 days of the date of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested or supply same in digital format to the Judge Pro Tem. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTIAN KENDALL VS. ZEN COMPOUND LLC, B/B/A TEMPLE NIGHT CLUB, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Stay Of Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANTS PAUL HEMMING AND TEMPLE NIGHT CLUB'S MOTION For Stay Of Proceedings. The motion is denied. First, defendant's motion has no evidence and defendant forgot to provide a copy of the rehabilitation and injunction order issued on November 7, 2013. Second, CCP 1064.9 does not apply to this case because it is not an "action or proceeding in the nature of an attachment, garnishment, or execution." In Hawthorne Savings F.S.B. v. Reliance Ins. Co. of Illinois (2005) 421 F.3d 835, 849 (amended and superseded by Hawthorne Savings F.S.B. v. Reliance Ins. Co. of Illinois (9th Cir. 2006) 433 F.3d 1089, the court explains that a purpose of a stay is to prohibit enforcement actions, not adjudication of the underlying merits. Third, the principle of full faith and credit does not apply because plaintiff was not a party to the Delaware proceedings. The Court received and considered defendants' late reply brief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: BRAD D SANDERS VS. EDWARD ZUSMAN, AN INDIVIDUAL ATTORNEY LICENSED TO et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT EDWARD ZUSMAN AND MARKUN, ZUSMAN, AND COMPTON, LLP'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled. Res judicata/collateral estoppel do not bar the fraud claim. See Business & Professions Code sec. 6204(e); Liska v. The Arns Law Firm (2004) 117 Cal.App.4th 275. Plaintiff's cause of action for fraud is well-pleaded. See paragraphs 10-18 & 50-51 of the Second Amended Complaint. rulings.Case Title: MARTIN SCOTT VS. PHAISAN AMATH YOUSUF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Pltf'S Mtn To Quash Def Luxor Cab Company'S Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, line 5, PLAINTIFF MARTIN SCOTT'S Notice Of Plaintiff's Motion To Quash Defendant Luxor Cab Company's Subpoenas Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff¿s motion to quash or limit the scope of medical subpoenas is granted. The language of the scope of the subpoenaed records is modified and limited to the medical care and treatment for the neck, arms/shoulders and lower back. Plaintiff¿s counsel is given a ¿first look¿ opportunity to determine if any documents, not within the scope of the subpoena, have been included by the health care providers. All subpoenaed documents produced by the deposition subpoena shall be delivered directly to the plaintiff ¿s attorney. The plaintiff ¿s attorney shall have 10 days to review the records and produce them to defense counsel along with a detailed log of anything removed or redacted (if any). If defense counsel disputes an entry in the privilege log the attorneys shall ¿meet and confer¿. If the parties cannot agree the Court will undertake an in camera review of the disputed records. The request for sanctions is denied. The parties¿ ¿meet and confer¿ efforts were undertaken in good faith. rulings.Case Title: CAROL A. TRUFANT VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. Defendant's motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted without leave to amend as to causes of action 2-4 (abuse of power, undue influence, and fraud). Plaintiff fails to allege a statutory basis for those claims against Defendant, who is a public entity. See Eastburn v. Regional Fire Protection Authority (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1175, 1183; Zelig v. County of Los Angeles (2002) 27 Cal.4th 1112, 1132. The motion is granted without leave to amend as to cause of action 1. Plaintiff does not allege that her Osteopenia is a disability or how the cart accommodates that condition. rulings.Case Title: ERNEST BATISTE JR VS. WILMA R. BATISTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Wilma Batiste'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, Line 23, Defendant Wilma Batiste'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint. Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has not, and cannot, cure fact that Ernest Batiste Jr. "Living Trust" has no capacity to sue. (Weil & Brown, Cal. Prac. Guide Civ. Pro. Before Trial Ch. 2-B; see also Galdjie v. Darwish (2003) 113 Cal.App.4th 1331, 1344.)) Defendant's demurrer is timely as it was filed 35 calendar days after Plaintiff was served by mail on November 4, 2013. (See CCP § 586(a)(1); CCP § 1005(b).) rulings.Case Title: FRANCENE KELLEY KENNEDY VS. REGINALD KELLEY et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT REGINALD KELLEY'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained pursuant to CCP sec. 430.10(c). Defendants' request for judicial notice is granted. The case is stayed pending the outcome of Carideo v. Kelley et al., San Francisco Superior Court Case No. CGC-12-518651. It serves judicial economy to first determine the issue already pending in the Carideo case, set for trial in June 2014, as to whether there are defenses to enforcement of the promissory note that is the subject of this collection action. rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL G. SCHINNER VS. RAYMOND ZHANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT HERSHORN & HENRY, LLP'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer is overruled as to each cause of action. All requests for judicial notice are granted. Defendant has not shown, as a matter of law, that the causes of action are barred by the litigation privilege. Further, this issue was addressed in the Court's order of September 27, 2013, which denied Defendant's special motion to strike. Defendant's demurrer to the second cause of action is overruled. Whether Plaintiff is an intended third party beneficiary to the Settlement Agreement involves a question of fact which is not a proper subject on a demurrer. Defendant has cited no applicable authority in its moving or reply papers that would bar Plaintiff from bringing a breach of contract against another alleged third party beneficiary to the Settlement Agreement. Defendant's demurrer to the third cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to state a cause of action for intentional interference of a contract against Defendant. Defendant's demurrer to the fourth cause of action is overruled. Whether Plaintiff has a possessory interest in the settlement funds is a question of fact which is not proper subject on demurrer. Defendant's demurrer on the grounds of uncertainty is overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL G. SCHINNER VS. RAYMOND ZHANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANTS CJH & ASSOCIATES AND CHIJEH HU'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer is overruled. All requests for judicial notice are granted. Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action is overruled. Although Plaintiff has not pled facts demonstrating the existence of a valid attorney's lien in accordance with California Rules of Professional Conduct 3-300, Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to establish Defendants' breach of the Settlement Agreement, to which Plaintiff is an intended third party beneficiary. (See First Amended Complaint 22-25, Exh. 4; see also Quelimane Co., Inc. v. Stewart Title Guar. Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26, 38-39 (holding a properly pled cause of action under any theory is good against general demurrer.)) Defendants' demurrer to the fourth cause of action is overruled. Whether Plaintiff has a right to possess the settlement funds is factual question which is not a proper subject on demurrer. rulings.Case Title: ROBIN A. KAY VS. JOHN W. DALTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, Line 18, DEFENDANT JOHN DALTON'S MOTION For Change OF Venue. Denied. Defendant has not met his burden of "negating the propriety of venue as laid on all possible grounds." (Karson Industries Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (1969) 273 Cal.App.2d 7, 8-9.) Defendant's moving papers do not address the breach of contract cause of action. (See Defendant's Memorandum of Points and Authorities, p. 1, lines 15-16.) Additionally, the complaint is founded on a contract, as all of the causes of action arise out of Defendants' alleged breach of the employment agreements and fee provisions. As such, pursuant to CCP sec. 395(a), venue is proper in the county where the contracts were entered into. Plaintiff has submitted evidence that the contracts were entered into in San Francisco County. (Declaration of Robin Kay, 6-9.) Defendant has submitted no evidence refuting this contention. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: INFINITE TRAVEL GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. JEFFREY VEINSHTEYN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 30, 2014, Line 20, DEFENDANTS VITTORIO FRONCILLO AND GAROLD KHAZAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the first, second, third, fifth, sixth, seventh, tenth, eleventh and twelfth causes of action. Overruled as to the fourth, eighth and ninth causes of action. For the first cause of action, Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege a fiduciary relationship with its insurance agent Defendant Khazan. (Kotlar v. Hartford Fire Ins. Co. (2000) 83 Cal.App.4th 1116, 1123.) For the second, third, fifth, sixth, seventh, tenth and eleventh causes of action, the claims are preempted by Civ. Code § 3426.7, subd. (b). These claims are 'based on the same nucleus of facts as the misappropriation of trade secrets claim for relief." (K.C. Multimedia, Inc. v. Bank of America Technology & Operations, Inc. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 939, 958.) Where based on the same nucleus of facts, preemption under this statute applies to both UCL and interference with contract claims. (Id. at p. 960 - 962.) For the fourth cause of action, Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege misappropriation of trade secrets. (CACI no. 4401.) The pleadings are not uncertain. For the eighth and ninth causes of action, Defendants withdraw their demurrer. (Reply, p. 6:21-25.) For the twelfth cause of action, Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege a relationship between the parties or other circumstances that require an accounting in equity.
